key_insight: S-Adam stabilizes non-smooth deep learning optimization by detecting local geometric instability and damping updates when the loss landscape is highly irregular.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical optimizer for quantization-aware training and other non-smooth workloads where standard adaptive methods can oscillate or fail to converge reliably.
method: Estimate Local Geometric Instability (LGI) from the variance of randomized directional derivatives.
Use an exponential damping factor exp(-λρ) to shrink steps in unstable regions.
Retain Adam-like fast progress in smoother parts of the landscape.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved performance on QAT and noisy small-batch training, with up to 6% higher accuracy on CIFAR-100 and 3% on TinyImageNet versus AdamW and Prox-SGD.
watch_item: LGI estimation adds extra stochastic probes, so the runtime overhead and sensitivity to probe settings may matter in large-scale training.
paper_title: Singularity-aware Optimization via Randomized Geometric Probing: Towards Stable Non-smooth Optimization
